title: WorkshopLogEvent
description: Cross-language log event contract for all Workshop services (Python/Rust/Go). Ensures consistent field names so request_id can be traced end-to-end across service boundaries.

ts.description: ISO 8601 datetime with timezone offset (UTC+8 preferred). Example: '2026-05-17T14:30:00.123+08:00'

level.description: Log severity level. Python logging names used as canonical form.

msg.description: Human-readable log message. Should be static; dynamic values go in separate fields.

service.description: Top-level service name. Identifies which process emitted this log.

request_id.description: 12 lowercase hex chars from X-Request-ID header. Traces a single HTTP request across services.

user_id.description: UUID v7 of the authenticated user. Null for unauthenticated or system requests.

space_id.description: UUID v7 of the active space/tenant. Null for cross-space or system-level logs.

module.description: Workshop domain module name if applicable.

duration_ms.description: Elapsed time in milliseconds for the operation being logged.

status_code.description: HTTP response status code for request-scoped logs.

path.description: HTTP request path (without query string) for request-scoped logs.

traceback.description: Full stack trace string from Python/Rust/Go. Include when level is ERROR or CRITICAL.
examples:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ...
ValueError: ...

error_type.description: Exception or error class name for programmatic error classification.

extra.description: Free-form object for service-specific fields not covered by the standard schema.
